summary: Replaced the handwritten assembly implementation of HSD_IDRemoveByIDFromTable in src/sysdolphin/baselib/id.c with matching C. The function removes an IDEntry from an HSD_IDTable bucket chain, falls back to default_table when the table argument is NULL, unlinks either the head or an interior node, frees the removed entry through HSD_ObjFree using hsd_iddata, and returns after the first match.
what_changed: Removed the asm body for HSD_IDRemoveByIDFromTable.
Added a C implementation of HSD_IDRemoveByIDFromTable.
Added an inline helper IDEntryFree(IDEntry* entry) that calls HSD_ObjFree(&hsd_iddata, entry).
Preserved the NULL table fallback behavior by assigning table = &default_table.
Handled both linked-list unlink cases: updating prev->next for non-head entries and updating table->table[hash(id)] for head entries.
Stops after freeing the first matching entry.
smart_moves: Factored the free call into an inline IDEntryFree helper, matching the original HSD_ObjFree(&hsd_iddata, entry) call while keeping the removal logic readable.
Used explicit prev and entry pointers to mirror the original linked-list traversal and make head-vs-non-head unlink behavior clear.
Kept the hash(id) expression in the bucket access, preserving the compiler's modulo/hash codegen behavior seen in the removed assembly.
Placed the matched C under the existing #pragma peephole on region, which likely mattered for reproducing the original instruction selection.
decomp_lessons: For hash-table removal functions, model the assembly as ordinary chained-bucket traversal with a prev pointer rather than trying to preserve label structure.
When an asm function repeatedly computes or uses a bucket derived from an ID, a named hash(id) helper can express the operation cleanly and still match.
Small inline wrappers around allocator/free APIs can help produce the desired call shape while documenting the object pool being used.
Default global table fallback is part of HSD ID table API behavior and should be preserved in matched C implementations.
naming_conventions: Uses HSD_ prefix for public baselib API function HSD_IDRemoveByIDFromTable.
Uses IDEntry as the internal linked-list node type.
Uses IDEntryFree as an internal helper name for freeing an IDEntry through hsd_iddata.
Uses default_table and hsd_iddata globals already present in the file.
Local variables are simple role names: entry and prev.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Converted a nofralloc asm function into structured C while preserving the same control flow semantics.
The original assembly's magic multiply sequence corresponds to hash(id), used as table->table[hash(id)].
The branch that checked whether the previous pointer was NULL maps to the two unlink cases: update the bucket head or update prev->next.
The original HSD_ObjFree call with hsd_iddata is represented by IDEntryFree(entry).
Use of #pragma peephole on is retained immediately before this matched C region.
follow_up_queries: Find other HSD ID table functions in src/sysdolphin/baselib/id.c that can reuse the hash(id) and default_table patterns.
Search for IDEntryFree or similar HSD_ObjFree(&hsd_iddata, entry) wrappers in later PRs.
Compare HSD_IDRemoveByIDFromTable with HSD_IDGetDataFromTable and HSD_IDInsertToTable for shared table traversal conventions.
